sub_instance.problem_statement:
Coastal Emergency scenario:
Storm season is coming. You need to wire a resilient emergency communication network across coastal villages.

You may choose any subset of the available cable links, but the chosen cable network must be a single connected tree that includes every required terminal village.
Every village that lies in your chosen network is active and must be assigned a radio frequency.
If an interference pair is active on both ends, those two villages must use different frequencies.

Objective:
total_cost = sum(cable costs used) + 15 * num_frequencies_used

Villages:
- Port, Bay, Cliff, Pine, Rock, Dune, Mill, Cape

Required terminals:
- Bay, Rock, Mill

Available cable links:
- Bay-Port: cost 12
- Bay-Cliff: cost 19
- Bay-Pine: cost 7
- Bay-Rock: cost 16
- Bay-Dune: cost 8
- Cliff-Port: cost 26
- Cliff-Pine: cost 22
- Cliff-Dune: cost 17
- Cliff-Mill: cost 10
- Pine-Port: cost 5
- Dune-Port: cost 20
- Dune-Pine: cost 15
- Dune-Rock: cost 9
- Dune-Mill: cost 13
- Mill-Rock: cost 17
- Cape-Cliff: cost 13
- Cape-Rock: cost 13
- Cape-Dune: cost 10
- Cape-Mill: cost 4

Interference pairs:
- Bay / Port
- Bay / Pine
- Pine / Port
- Dune / Rock
- Cape / Cliff
- Cape / Pine
- Cape / Rock
- Cape / Dune

Rules:
- Your cable choice must be a tree, not a cyclic network.
- All required terminals must lie in one connected component.
- Frequencies matter only for active villages in the chosen network.
- If you stop immediately or fail to produce a valid answer, the baseline below is what gets scored.

Baseline total cost: 67
Baseline answer summary:

Active villages: Bay, Rock, Dune, Mill
Frequencies: Bay=1, Rock=1, Dune=2, Mill=1
Edge cost: 37
Frequencies used: 2
Total cost: 67

sub_instance.problem_statement:
You run a two-factory manufacturing pipeline. Factory A makes components, then Factory B finishes the same jobs.
Each factory is its own job shop with one-job-per-machine exclusivity and no preemption.
Every job must finish all of its Factory A operations before that same job may start any Factory B operation.
Your objective is to minimize the coupled makespan: the time when the final Factory B operation finishes.

Instance size: 5 jobs, 6 machines in each factory.
Jobs and ordered operations:
- Job 1: Factory A MA2 (5) -> MA1 (4) -> MA4 (5) -> MA6 (2) -> MA5 (6) -> MA3 (9); Factory B MB4 (3) -> MB6 (4) -> MB2 (3) -> MB5 (4) -> MB3 (4) -> MB1 (6)
- Job 2: Factory A MA5 (4) -> MA6 (7) -> MA2 (9) -> MA3 (6) -> MA4 (9) -> MA1 (5); Factory B MB6 (5) -> MB3 (5) -> MB5 (9) -> MB1 (2) -> MB2 (5) -> MB4 (8)
- Job 3: Factory A MA5 (4) -> MA6 (5) -> MA2 (9) -> MA4 (6) -> MA1 (9) -> MA3 (8); Factory B MB2 (2) -> MB4 (8) -> MB6 (3) -> MB5 (8) -> MB3 (3) -> MB1 (8)
- Job 4: Factory A MA1 (7) -> MA2 (7) -> MA6 (3) -> MA3 (7) -> MA5 (7) -> MA4 (3); Factory B MB6 (5) -> MB4 (7) -> MB1 (6) -> MB5 (3) -> MB3 (6) -> MB2 (6)
- Job 5: Factory A MA6 (9) -> MA5 (9) -> MA3 (9) -> MA1 (8) -> MA2 (9) -> MA4 (2); Factory B MB5 (1) -> MB4 (5) -> MB2 (7) -> MB1 (7) -> MB6 (8) -> MB3 (9)

Constraints:
- A machine handles at most one job at a time.
- Each job must respect its listed operation order inside each factory.
- Factory B job Jx cannot start until Factory A job Jx has fully completed.
- When you emit a schedule, every required operation must appear exactly once.

A deterministic feasible baseline schedule is provided below. If you stop immediately or fail to produce a valid schedule, this baseline is what gets scored.
Baseline makespan: 178
Baseline schedule summary:
Factory A: MA1: J1[5-9],J2[57-62],J3[63-72],J4[72-79],J5[121-129]; MA2: J1[0-5],J2[33-42],J3[42-51],J4[79-86],J5[129-138]; MA3: J1[22-31],J2[42-48],J3[72-80],J4[89-96],J5[112-121]; MA4: J1[9-14],J2[48-57],J3[57-63],J4[103-106],J5[138-140]; MA5: J1[16-22],J2[22-26],J3[26-30],J4[96-103],J5[103-112]; MA6: J1[14-16],J2[26-33],J3[33-38],J4[86-89],J5[89-98]
Factory B: MB1: J1[49-55],J2[81-83],J3[118-126],J4[126-132],J5[154-161]; MB2: J1[38-41],J2[83-88],J3[88-90],J4[141-147],J5[147-154]; MB3: J1[45-49],J2[67-72],J3[115-118],J4[135-141],J5[169-178]; MB4: J1[31-34],J2[88-96],J3[96-104],J4[112-119],J5[141-146]; MB5: J1[41-45],J2[72-81],J3[107-115],J4[132-135],J5[140-141]; MB6: J1[34-38],J2[62-67],J3[104-107],J4[107-112],J5[161-169]
Makespan: 178

sub_instance.problem_statement:
You run a two-factory manufacturing pipeline. Factory A makes components, then Factory B finishes the same jobs.
Each factory is its own job shop with one-job-per-machine exclusivity and no preemption.
Every job must finish all of its Factory A operations before that same job may start any Factory B operation.
Your objective is to minimize the coupled makespan: the time when the final Factory B operation finishes.

Instance size: 6 jobs, 7 machines in each factory.
Jobs and ordered operations:
- Job 1: Factory A MA2 (4) -> MA1 (5) -> MA4 (2) -> MA3 (6) -> MA6 (9) -> MA5 (3) -> MA7 (4); Factory B MB6 (3) -> MB4 (4) -> MB2 (4) -> MB7 (6) -> MB5 (9) -> MB3 (4) -> MB1 (8)
- Job 2: Factory A MA2 (5) -> MA6 (9) -> MA3 (2) -> MA5 (5) -> MA1 (8) -> MA7 (9) -> MA4 (3); Factory B MB2 (8) -> MB6 (3) -> MB4 (4) -> MB3 (4) -> MB5 (7) -> MB7 (3) -> MB1 (7)
- Job 3: Factory A MA5 (8) -> MA4 (3) -> MA7 (8) -> MA1 (2) -> MA3 (4) -> MA6 (2) -> MA2 (9); Factory B MB5 (6) -> MB3 (4) -> MB7 (7) -> MB4 (9) -> MB1 (9) -> MB2 (1) -> MB6 (3)
- Job 4: Factory A MA7 (3) -> MA6 (6) -> MA1 (6) -> MA4 (5) -> MA2 (3) -> MA3 (7) -> MA5 (9); Factory B MB4 (1) -> MB7 (7) -> MB2 (3) -> MB3 (3) -> MB6 (7) -> MB5 (1) -> MB1 (9)
- Job 5: Factory A MA5 (3) -> MA7 (3) -> MA4 (6) -> MA1 (5) -> MA6 (8) -> MA2 (1) -> MA3 (2); Factory B MB6 (6) -> MB4 (9) -> MB7 (3) -> MB5 (9) -> MB2 (3) -> MB1 (4) -> MB3 (5)
- Job 6: Factory A MA1 (6) -> MA2 (3) -> MA5 (7) -> MA6 (7) -> MA7 (9) -> MA4 (7) -> MA3 (9); Factory B MB5 (1) -> MB3 (5) -> MB1 (7) -> MB6 (1) -> MB2 (5) -> MB7 (7) -> MB4 (5)

Constraints:

sub_instance.answer_contract: Object with "factory_a", "factory_b", and "makespan". Each factory maps machine names to lists of ["J<id>", start, end] triples.
sub_instance.problem_statement:
You run a two-factory manufacturing pipeline. Factory A makes components, then Factory B finishes the same jobs.
Each factory is its own job shop with one-job-per-machine exclusivity and no preemption.
Every job must finish all of its Factory A operations before that same job may start any Factory B operation.
Your objective is to minimize the coupled makespan: the time when the final Factory B operation finishes.

Instance size: 6 jobs, 7 machines in each factory.
Jobs and ordered operations:
- Job 1: Factory A MA7 (8) -> MA3 (1) -> MA1 (4) -> MA5 (8) -> MA6 (1) -> MA2 (2) -> MA4 (2); Factory B MB4 (8) -> MB5 (7) -> MB3 (1) -> MB2 (9) -> MB6 (7) -> MB1 (6) -> MB7 (1)
- Job 2: Factory A MA2 (2) -> MA1 (8) -> MA5 (3) -> MA3 (8) -> MA4 (8) -> MA6 (3) -> MA7 (3); Factory B MB6 (3) -> MB3 (9) -> MB5 (6) -> MB7 (9) -> MB4 (4) -> MB2 (2) -> MB1 (2)
- Job 3: Factory A MA1 (3) -> MA4 (2) -> MA7 (2) -> MA3 (8) -> MA6 (4) -> MA2 (6) -> MA5 (9); Factory B MB1 (3) -> MB3 (2) -> MB6 (7) -> MB5 (3) -> MB2 (8) -> MB7 (1) -> MB4 (1)
- Job 4: Factory A MA6 (1) -> MA4 (7) -> MA7 (8) -> MA1 (4) -> MA5 (1) -> MA3 (2) -> MA2 (5); Factory B MB2 (8) -> MB6 (9) -> MB1 (4) -> MB4 (8) -> MB3 (5) -> MB5 (4) -> MB7 (2)
- Job 5: Factory A MA1 (3) -> MA5 (7) -> MA6 (2) -> MA2 (4) -> MA3 (9) -> MA7 (5) -> MA4 (7); Factory B MB1 (7) -> MB4 (5) -> MB2 (9) -> MB7 (9) -> MB5 (1) -> MB3 (5) -> MB6 (2)
- Job 6: Factory A MA1 (9) -> MA6 (8) -> MA2 (8) -> MA3 (5) -> MA4 (1) -> MA5 (6) -> MA7 (7); Factory B MB2 (1) -> MB6 (3) -> MB1 (7) -> MB3 (4) -> MB4 (1) -> MB5 (3) -> MB7 (8)

Constraints:
- A machine handles at most one job at a time.
- Each job must respect its listed operation order inside each factory.
- Factory B job Jx cannot start until Factory A job Jx has fully completed.
- When you emit a schedule, every required operation must appear exactly once.

A deterministic feasible baseline schedule is provided below. If you stop immediately or fail to produce a valid schedule, this baseline is what gets scored.
Baseline makespan: 203
Baseline schedule summary:
Factory A: MA1: J1[9-13],J2[26-34],J3[34-37],J4[89-93],J5[93-96],J6[96-105]; MA2: J1[22-24],J2[24-26],J3[73-79],J4[96-101],J5[105-109],J6[113-121]; MA3: J1[8-9],J2[37-45],J3[61-69],J4[94-96],J5[109-118],J6[121-126]; MA4: J1[24-26],J2[45-53],J3[53-55],J4[74-81],J5[123-130],J6[130-131]; MA5: J1[13-21],J2[34-37],J3[79-88],J4[93-94],J5[96-103],J6[131-137]; MA6: J1[21-22],J2[53-56],J3[69-73],J4[73-74],J5[103-105],J6[105-113]; MA7: J1[0-8],J2[56-59],J3[59-61],J4[81-89],J5[118-123],J6[137-144]
Factory B: MB1: J1[58-64],J2[92-94],J3[94-97],J4[134-138],J5[138-145],J6[180-187]; MB2: J1[42-51],J2[90-92],J3[109-117],J4[117-125],J5[151-160],J6[160-161]; MB3: J1[41-42],J2[62-71],J3[97-99],J4[146-151],J5[170-175],J6[187-191]; MB4: J1[26-34],J2[86-90],J3[118-119],J4[138-146],J5[146-151],J6[191-192]; MB5: J1[34-41],J2[71-77],J3[106-109],J4[151-155],J5[169-170],J6[192-195]; MB6: J1[51-58],J2[59-62],J3[99-106],J4[125-134],J5[175-177],J6[177-180]; MB7: J1[64-65],J2[77-86],J3[117-118],J4[155-157],J5[160-169],J6[195-203]
Makespan: 203
